title: Umami Crunchy Chinese Sweet Potato Stir-fry
description: A diabetic-friendly Chinese dish featuring umami flavors and a crunchy texture from sweet potatoes in a savory sauce.

ingredients.name: Sweet potatoes
ingredients.notes: preferably steamed and thinly sliced for crispiness

ingredients.name: Fish sauce (optional)
ingredients.notes: for added umami and depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: optional for extra crunchiness and flavor

instructions: Preheat your wok or large frying pan over high heat. Make sure it's very hot before adding oil.
Slice the sweet potatoes into thin rounds, around 1/8 inch thick, and steam them for about 5 minutes until they are tender but still crisp.
In a small bowl, mix soy sauce with any optional fish sauce if you're using it. Set aside.
Add sesame oil to the hot wok or pan, followed by sliced green onions and minced garlic. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the steamed sweet potatoes to the stir-fry and toss them in the onion-garlic mixture for another minute.
Pour the sauce over the sweet potatoes and mix well to coat them evenly. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle crushed peanuts on top of the sweet potatoes and scallions just before serving.
Serve hot with a side of steamed green vegetables or brown rice.
